about_meta_description: Discover Arkeonix Labs, the editorial arm dedicated to tech precision and veracity.
about_intro: Arkeonix Labs is an entity dedicated to exhaustive products and technology analysis. Our mission is to provide precise, technical, and verified information.
about_mission: Our mission is to set a quality standard in tech disclosure, providing analysis that allows professionals and enthusiasts to make decisions based on real data and technical experience.
about_topic_products_desc: In-depth technical reviews of components, servers, and tech gadgets.
about_topic_news_desc: Selected news analyzed under our technical precision prism.
about_topic_guides_desc: Deployment guides and infrastructure manuals for professional and home environments.
about_topic_reviews_desc: Performance tests executed in our lab under controlled conditions.
about_creator_intro: Arkeonix Labs was born from the need for a rigorous technical space. Led by David López, the project integrates software development with products engineering.
about_creator_experience: With a hybrid focus between code and silicon, we apply scientific rigor to every publication to guarantee maximum content utility.
about_value_quality: Rigor: Precision is our absolute priority.
about_value_honesty: Independence: Objective analysis free from commercial biases.
about_value_updated: Technical Relevance: Content always aligned with the latest industry evolutions.
about_value_community: Feedback: We value technical corrections and contributions from our audience.
about_contact_cta_desc: We are ready to meet your technical requirements.
privacy_intro: At Arkeonix Labs, privacy is an extension of our technical security. Here we detail how we protect your information.
privacy_data_collection_text: We only process essential data (email) for technical report delivery or direct support communications.
privacy_third_parties_text: We use secure infrastructure and comply with current regulations in third-party data processing.
privacy_rights_text: You can exercise your access or definitive deletion rights by contacting davidlopez00@proton.me.
privacy_contact_text: For legal or privacy matters, contact us at davidlopez00@proton.me.
terms_intro: Use of Arkeonix Labs implies acceptance of our navigation rules and technical content usage.
terms_usage_text: Technical content is provided for informational purposes. Its application in production environments is the user's responsibility.
terms_content_text: All intellectual material generated by Arkeonix Labs is protected. Redistribution requires express authorization.
terms_liability_text: Arkeonix Labs is not responsible for products or software failures derived from applying our guides.
terms_changes_text: Operational terms may be adjusted to reflect changes in our technical service delivery.
terms_contact_text: For contractual or legal questions, use the davidlopez00@proton.me channel.
cookies_intro: We explain how we manage cookies to optimize your technical experience on our site.
cookies_what_text: Cookies are technical identifiers that allow us to remember your preferences and improve site stability.
cookies_types_text: We use critical session cookies and analytical cookies to measure technical report performance.
cookies_management_text: You can revoke cookie consent from your browser settings at any time.
cookies_third_text: Some traffic analysis tools may deposit cookies to generate anonymous usage statistics.
cookies_changes_text: This protocol is periodically reviewed to comply with web security standards.
cookies_banner_text: We use technical cookies to ensure the precision of your navigation on Arkeonix Labs.
affiliate_intro: Arkeonix Labs participates in affiliate programs, including the Amazon Associates program. This means some links in our posts are affiliate links.
affiliate_what_text: An affiliate link is a special link to a product or service. If you click it and make a purchase, Arkeonix Labs receives a small commission from the seller. This mechanism helps us maintain the site and continue publishing technical content.
affiliate_how_text: When we publish a product analysis that includes a link to Amazon or another retailer, that link may be an affiliate link. The commission we receive comes from the seller, not from you. You pay exactly the same price as if you had accessed the product directly.
affiliate_editorial_text: Participation in affiliate programs does not influence our editorial analysis. Our evaluations are based on the product's specifications, buyer feedback, and our technical judgment. We do not accept payments from manufacturers in exchange for favorable evaluations.
affiliate_cost_text: No. Affiliate links do not add any extra cost to your purchase. The price you pay is always the same, regardless of whether you reach the product through our link or directly.
affiliate_contact_text: If you have questions about our affiliate policy, you can reach us at davidlopez00@proton.me.
footer_tagline: The excellence standard in technical precision.
arkeonix_meta_description: Arkeonix SaaS is a production-ready boilerplate with authentication, Stripe payments, multi-tenancy, RBAC, audit logs and much more. Launch your SaaS in days, not months.
arkeonix_hero_subheading: Enterprise-grade boilerplate with Next.js 15, Prisma, Auth.js, Stripe, and Resend. Authentication, payments, and emails — ready in minutes. No lock-in. Full source code.
arkeonix_features_subtitle: 12 critical modules already built and tested, so you can focus on your product.
arkeonix_feature_auth_desc: OAuth with GitHub and Google, passwordless magic links, JWT sessions in HttpOnly cookies. Edge-compatible and secure.
arkeonix_feature_rbac_desc: Role-based access control enforced in Edge middleware. Platform roles (USER, ADMIN) and org roles (MEMBER, ADMIN, OWNER).
arkeonix_feature_billing_desc: Checkout, customer portal, and idempotent webhooks. Three plans with server-side enforced limits. MRR calculated in the admin panel.
arkeonix_feature_teams_desc: Invite members by email, assign roles, revoke access. Pending invitation system with expiring tokens.
arkeonix_feature_apikeys_desc: Generate, revoke, and monitor API keys. SHA-256 hashing, never stored in plain text. Per-plan limits enforced.
arkeonix_feature_email_desc: 5 React Email templates: welcome, invitation, subscription created, canceled, and payment failed. Sent via Resend.
arkeonix_feature_admin_desc: KPI dashboard with Recharts charts and paginated audit log. Restricted to ADMIN and SUPER_ADMIN roles.
arkeonix_feature_i18n_desc: English and Spanish included with next-intl v4. Add new languages in 4 steps. Localized URLs.
arkeonix_feature_darkmode_desc: Detects system preference, persists across sessions. Toggle in the sidebar. Implemented with next-themes.
arkeonix_feature_cicd_desc: 5 GitHub Actions workflows (lint, test, E2E, build, deploy). Multi-stage Dockerfile for production. Docker Compose for local dev.
arkeonix_feature_ratelimit_desc: 4 Upstash Redis limiters: auth, API, strict mode, and webhooks. Per-IP or user-ID. Returns 429 with Retry-After.
arkeonix_feature_audit_desc: Immutable action log: actor, resource, organization, IP, and User-Agent. Compliance-ready. Fire-and-forget with Sentry fallback.
arkeonix_demo_subtitle: Explore the live demo of the boilerplate. Everything you see is included in the source code.
arkeonix_included_subtitle: Full source code. No subscriptions. No hidden dependencies. Use the project for unlimited commercial projects.
arkeonix_included_note: Complete source code · No subscriptions · Commercial license included
arkeonix_included_nextjs: Complete Next.js 15 project with App Router and database migrations
arkeonix_included_auth: Auth.js v5 with OAuth (GitHub, Google) and passwordless magic links
arkeonix_included_stripe: Stripe checkout, idempotent webhooks, and customer portal
arkeonix_included_multitenant: Multi-tenancy with organizations, email invitations, and roles
arkeonix_included_limits: Per-plan limits enforced server-side (orgs, members, API keys)
arkeonix_included_admin: Admin panel with KPIs, charts, and paginated audit log
arkeonix_included_ratelimit: Rate limiting with Upstash Redis in 4 layers
arkeonix_included_i18n: i18n pre-configured (EN + ES), extensible to any language
arkeonix_included_darkmode: Dark mode with next-themes and system preference detection
arkeonix_included_sentry: Sentry configured with Session Replay and source maps
arkeonix_included_cicd: 5 GitHub Actions workflows: lint, test, E2E, build, and deploy
arkeonix_included_docker: Multi-stage Dockerfile + Docker Compose for local development
arkeonix_included_audit: Immutable audit logs with Sentry fallback
arkeonix_pricing_subtitle: Choose the plan that best fits your project. Pay once, use forever.
arkeonix_pricing_note: One-time purchase · No subscriptions · Commercial license · Updates included
arkeonix_plan_starter_desc: Launch your first SaaS with everything you need from day 1.
arkeonix_plan_pro_desc: For agencies and teams shipping multiple products. Best value on the market.
arkeonix_contact_subtitle: Fill out the form and we'll get back to you within 24 hours.
arkeonix_contact_message_placeholder: Tell us about your project and which plan you're interested in...
arkeonix_reviews_subtitle: Real feedback from developers and teams already using it.
arkeonix_review1_text: I'd been trying to set up auth with Prisma and Next.js 15 for weeks. With this boilerplate I had it running in an afternoon. Multi-tenancy comes pre-built — something that would normally take me days.
arkeonix_review2_text: What surprised me most is that it's not a rigid template. It's your code from the first commit. No weird dependencies, no lock-in. Stripe and Resend integrated and working in minutes.
arkeonix_review3_text: For an agency that ships recurring projects, the Pro plan pays for itself on the first project. Having RBAC and audit logs already implemented saved us weeks of work.
arkeonix_author_title: Built by someone who uses it every day
arkeonix_author_bio1: I'm David, a QA Automation Engineer focused on infrastructure and DevOps tooling. By day I automate tests with Java and Selenium — but when I close my work laptop, I open it again to manage my private server with Docker, explore new dev tools, and ship personal projects from scratch.
arkeonix_author_bio2: I built this boilerplate as a foundation for my own SaaS ideas. After years of research I kept hitting the same wall: hours spent configuring auth, payments and permissions — things that should already be solved. So I solved them properly, once, and made it available to anyone who shares the same frustration.
arkeonix_arch_subtitle: Clean, modular structure ready for production from the first commit.
arkeonix_arch_desc: Each layer is separated by responsibility. No business logic mixed with UI, no scattered configuration across the codebase.
arkeonix_arch_item1_desc: Next.js App Router with nested layouts, auth middleware and per-tenant separation.
arkeonix_arch_item2_desc: Server Actions, Prisma queries and Zod validation. Client and server completely decoupled.
arkeonix_arch_item3_desc: Reusable components with shadcn/ui, Tailwind and variants defined in one place.
arkeonix_arch_item4_desc: Auth.js, Stripe, Resend and Prisma configured and ready to use from day 1.
arkeonix_arch_item5_desc: Schema with User, Organization, Membership, AuditLog and ApiKey. Migrations included.
arkeonix_footer_cta_subtitle: Join teams building enterprise SaaS with Arkeonix.
arkeonix_footer_note: One-time purchase · Unlimited projects · Commercial license included
arkeonix_thanks_message: Thank you for purchasing the Arkeonix boilerplate. You'll receive an email shortly with the next steps.
arkeonix_thanks_step1: Check your inbox — we've sent you an email with access to the GitHub repository.
arkeonix_thanks_step2: Accept the invitation to the private GitHub repository to clone the code.
arkeonix_thanks_step3: Follow the installation guide in the README to get your environment running in minutes.

guia_review1_text: I had been sending CVs for months with no response. The guide helped me understand what companies were really looking for. Three weeks after applying what it teaches, I had my first offer.
guia_review1_role: Junior QA Automation — First offer in 3 weeks
guia_review2_text: I thought I needed 5 years of experience for my first tech job. The guide showed me how to build a portfolio that competes with more senior profiles. I wish I'd had it sooner.
guia_review2_role: Recent DAM graduate — Junior at a product company
guia_review3_text: I came from hospitality and had no idea where to start. The chapter on what to learn and in what order saved me months of studying things no interview would ever ask me.
guia_review3_role: Career changer — Now a junior developer at a startup

academia_pricing_desc: One payment. Full access to all exams in all categories, forever.
academia_reviews_title: What people say about preparing their certifications with it
academia_review1_text: I passed the ISTQB Foundation on my first try. The practice exams are very similar to the real ones in difficulty and format. The explanations for each answer are the most valuable part.
academia_review1_role: QA Automation Engineer — ISTQB Foundation Level
academia_review2_text: I did over 200 questions before the exam. The detailed explanations helped me understand the 'why', not just memorize answers. That makes all the difference.
academia_review2_role: Junior QA Tester — Preparing ISTQB certification
academia_review3_text: I use it to stay up to date on best practices. The content on agile testing and methodologies is especially useful for senior-level technical interviews.
academia_review3_role: Senior QA Engineer — 6 years in the industry
